section_summaries.heading: RL Problem Setup
section_summaries.summary: Frames reinforcement learning as an intelligent agent interacting with an environment, receiving rewards and updating its policy. Introduces Markov Decision Processes.

section_summaries.heading: Returns and Discounting
section_summaries.summary: Defines the discounted return model. The discount rate (between 0 and 1) bounds the infinite sum of rewards and favors earlier rewards, encouraging the agent to find shorter paths to its goal.

section_summaries.heading: Value Functions and Q-Learning
section_summaries.summary: Introduces state-value and action-value functions, and presents Q-learning as a model-free temporal-difference method, along with the exploration vs exploitation trade-off.

overall_summary: This document presents Reinforcement Learning as a third major learning paradigm complementing the supervised methods covered earlier. An intelligent agent interacts with an environment modeled as a Markov Decision Process, choosing actions according to a policy and receiving scalar rewards. The discounted return model is introduced: a discount rate between 0 and 1 bounds the infinite sum of future rewards and gives the agent a preference for earlier rewards, which in practice encourages it to find shorter paths to its goal. The document then defines state-value and action-value functions, and presents Q-learning as a canonical model-free, temporal-difference method that learns Q-values from experience. The exploration versus exploitation trade-off is discussed as a defining challenge of RL, tying the chapter back to the agent and search material from the start of the course.
